content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic motivation mechanisms (from Topic A's ICM framework for autonomous agents) can be repurposed to solve the critical unaddressed problem of active memory pruning and retention in 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agents. Instead of using curiosity only to guide exploration of new environments, we can frame a curious agent's intrinsic reward signal as a metric to prioritize which new experiences to encode into long-term memory and which to discard, aligning memory curation with the agent's own learning goals.
